Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: force TERM=dumb for gh auth PTY, extend timeout to 60s

gh auth login uses bubbletea TUI which emits cursor-positioning
ANSI sequences. With TERM set to a capable value, the fancy UI
draws over itself with escape codes that our sanitizer can't
reliably reconstruct into recognisable strings like
'Login with a web browser'. Setting TERM=dumb + NO_COLOR=1 forces
gh into plain-text fallback mode so the prompt text appears as
literal printable characters.

Also:
- Extend code wait deadline from 30s to 60s
- Log PTY OSErrors at WARNING instead of silently swallowing them
- Dump the sanitised PTY buffer in the timeout warning so failures
  are visible without needing DEBUG logging enabled
- Broaden Stage-1 match: 'web browser' (case-insensitive) in
  addition to exact phrase matches
- Add per-iteration DEBUG log of PTY content for future diagnosis

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: implement real Copilot API call in /api/ask (0.1.1)

Replace the hardcoded stub response with an actual call to the
GitHub Copilot chat completions API (api.githubcopilot.com) —
the same API that powers the GitHub Copilot CLI.

How it works:
1. The stored GitHub OAuth token (from the auth flow) is exchanged
   for a short-lived Copilot API token via
   api.github.com/copilot_internal/v2/token (cached, auto-refreshed).
2. The prompt + system prompt are sent to
   api.githubcopilot.com/chat/completions (OpenAI-compatible).
3. If a Home Assistant MCP server URL is configured, it is included
   in the system prompt so the model knows about it.
4. The real response text is returned to HA Assistant/Assist.

Also:
- Increase api.py ask timeout from 10s to 120s (LLM calls take time)
- Bump to 0.1.1

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: switch gh auth to device code flow (remove --web)

gh auth login --web forces a PKCE web flow with a localhost redirect
that can never complete inside a headless container. Switch to the
standard interactive device code flow used by GitHub Copilot CLI:

  gh auth login --hostname <host> --git-protocol https ...

The PTY driver now uses a two-stage state machine:
  Stage 1 - detect the 'How would you like to authenticate?' prompt
            and press Enter to select 'Login with a web browser'
  Stage 2 - wait for the device code (XXXX-XXXX), capture it, then
            press Enter so gh opens https://github.com/login/device

The user navigates to github.com/login/device, enters the code, and
gh polls for completion — no localhost redirect needed.

Bump to 0.1.0.6.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: clear stale pending auth state with null user_code

Three places were preventing the auth code from ever appearing:

1. Bridge startup: if the persisted auth state file contained a
   pending_device_flow with user_code=null (left from a previous
   crashed/timed-out attempt), it was restored and surfaced to HA
   as 'already pending'. Now cleared on startup.

2. Bridge /auth/device/start: if the gh process was running but
   produced no code, the endpoint returned the null-code state
   unchanged instead of restarting. Now kills and restarts.

3. config_flow: a pending state with user_code=None was treated as
   a valid 'already pending' flow, so /auth/device/start was never
   called. Now only reuses a pending state that has a real code.

Bump to 0.1.0.5.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: wait for one-time code before pressing Enter in gh auth login

The gh auth login --web flow prints the one-time device code first,
then waits for Enter before opening the browser. Previously, Enter was
sent immediately after spawning the process, before any output was
read. This caused the code to never be captured, so the HA UI showed
no code or verification URL.

Fix: poll gh output for up to 20 seconds waiting for the code to
appear. Once captured, send Enter so gh proceeds with the auth flow.
If no code appears within the timeout, send Enter anyway and let
subsequent polls pick it up.

Also bump versions to 0.1.0.4.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat(redesign): premium visual overhaul, number reconciliation, performance fix

Visual:
- Hero carousel: SVG chevron arrows (w-12 h-12), gradient CTA buttons
- Typography: eliminated all sub-10px text, minimum now text-xs (12px)
- Sub-pages: unique color-identity hero banners (cyan/mercado, green/tech, purple/pesquisa, red/seguranca)
- Panorama: larger KPIs, larger section headers, premium glass styling

Numbers reconciled:
- Countries: 972→37 (filter ≥20 jobs, exclude garbage)
- Insights: 104→46 (only publishable)
- Sources: 85→36 (real count)
- Removed fake 'Capital Rastreado US$ 1.4T' KPI (funding data is mis-sourced)
- Replaced with 'Tech Trends' KPI using real tech_trends data (38K signals)
- Fixed '~85% EUA' to '~54% EUA'
- Patents filtered: 37K mock-generated excluded

Methodology page:
- Replaced fake source list (CrunchBase, PitchBook, LinkedIn API) with real connected sources
- Added REAL/MOCK badges per KPI (10 real, 10 mock)
- Added honesty disclaimer about unconnected sources

Performance:
- Created materialized view mv_papers_vs_jobs (85s query→<100ms)
- Charts API now returns in <1s instead of timing out

Panorama charts:
- Replaced empty 'Investimento por Tipo' panel with 'Geopolítica ACLED' (929K events)

Fix backendUrl context loss in 2-phase SQL queries and unify Emoji Reaction client selection (#379)

* Initial plan

* Fix backendUrl context loss in 2-phase SQL queries and unify Emoji Reaction client selection

Phase1 queries now capture backendUrl (which is correctly filtered by WHERE pb.backendUrl IN (...))
and override Phase2 results where MIN(pb.backendUrl) could return the wrong backend.

Emoji Reaction handlers in Actions.tsx and EmojiReactions.tsx now use status.appIndex
(the account that received the status) instead of selectedAppIndex (the posting account),
making them consistent with Favourite/Reblog/Bookmark actions.

Co-authored-by: BYVoid <245270+BYVoid@users.noreply.github.com>
Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/BYVoid/OpenCC/sessions/fe063606-4785-4a11-ba16-6dcbdaaa7c80

* Drop Python 3.8 support: incompatible with setuptools>=78.1.1

setuptools 78.x requires Python >= 3.9, so building for Python 3.8
fails after the setuptools minimum was raised to 78.1.1. Remove 3.8
from the CI test matrix and all release scripts (Linux, macOS, Windows),
and update the twine upload environments from py3.8 to py3.9.

---------

commit.message:
feat: build complete Arabic Club Management System

- Vite + React (JSX) frontend with Tailwind CSS
- Firebase Auth (email/password for leader/media, email link for department)
- Multi-role system: leader, media (with approval), department
- 15 pages: Home, LeaderLogin, DepartmentEnter, FinishSignIn, SignupMedia,
  PendingMediaApproval, Events, EventDetail, LeaderMembers, Operations,
  MyTasks, Notifications, Availability, Leaderboard, WeeklyLeaderboard
- Framer Motion animations throughout
- Lucide React icons
- react-hot-toast notifications
- Left sidebar layout, light theme
- Firestore + Storage integration (file attachments per event)
- Task generation from event requirements (poster/video/coverage)
- Points system via Cloud Functions (auto-award on task completion)
- Weekly leaderboard with Malaysia Time (UTC+8) week key
- Firestore security rules + Storage rules
- clsx for conditional classes

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: use git ls-remote for tag existence check in sync workflows

The previous git rev-parse check only looked locally, which fails with
shallow clones when the tag exists on the remote but not in the local
history. This could cause the sync job to fail, preventing the release
workflow from being triggered.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
[ci] F: Publish Windows release artifacts

Add a windows-release-archive job that packages Windows standalone
binaries (*.exe, *.elf, *.img) into a .zip archive and uploads it
as a release artifact alongside the existing Linux .tar.bz2 archives.
Update publish-latest to depend on the new job and update the
publish-release action to handle both .tar.bz2 and .zip assets with
the correct Content-Type headers.

Closes #1792

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
[ci] E: Unify ci-windows.yml into ci.yml

Move all Windows CI jobs (guest cross-compilation, source checks,
build + unit tests, and WHP integration tests) from the separate
ci-windows.yml workflow into ci.yml as a dedicated Windows CI
section. Wire up the precheck dependency and adopt the same gating
conditions as the existing Linux jobs. Delete ci-windows.yml.

Closes #1793

commit.message:
feat: Phase 0 scaffold — Electron + TypeScript + IPC contracts + security + CI

Closes #28 — electron-vite project scaffold with TypeScript strict mode
Closes #33 — typed IPC contracts in src/shared/types/ipc.ts (all channels + Zod schemas)
Closes #37 — Electron security: nodeIntegration:false, contextIsolation:true, sandbox:true, CSP
Closes #40 — GitHub Actions CI: unit/integration + E2E matrix (ubuntu/windows/macos)

Project structure:
  src/main/       — Electron main process (BrowserWindow security config, navigation guard)
  src/preload/    — contextBridge typed API (ElectronAPI interface, no raw ipcRenderer)
  src/renderer/   — React shell (Phase 1 layout to follow)
  src/shared/     — IPC channel constants, Zod schemas, ElectronAPI type

Security hardening (addresses PRD issues #3, #4, #5):
  - BrowserWindow: nodeIntegration:false, contextIsolation:true, sandbox:true
  - CSP header injected via session.webRequest.onHeadersReceived
  - will-navigate + setWindowOpenHandler block uncontrolled navigation
  - External URLs routed to shell.openExternal

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
[syscall] B: Fix readlink buffer bugs

safe::fs::readlink() had two bugs:

1. Used Vec::with_capacity(PATH_MAX) which creates a zero-length
   buffer, causing bufsiz=0 in the IPC request to linuxd. This
   corrupted nanvixd IPC state and caused subsequent VM tests to hang.
   buffer.

2. Used FileSystemPath::try_from_bytes() which expects NUL-terminated
   input (CString::from_vec_with_nul), but POSIX readlink() does not
   NUL-terminate its output. Fixed by converting via core::str::from_utf8
   and FileSystemPath::new().

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Polish README: fix grammar, jargon, passive voice, and descriptions

- 'not time to become' → 'who don't have time to become'
- 'items deployed' → 'everything deployed' (avoid Fabric jargon)
- 'the architecture' → 'an architecture' (13 task flows exist)
- Deploy row: passive → active voice to match other rows
- 'project-brief' → 'project brief' + em-dash for rhythm
- Item Registry: describe what the reader finds, not what the agent needs
- Templates: 'definition files' not 'starting points' (they're the real thing)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: fabric-task-flows
repository.description: Task flows let you describe your problem and have an AI agent generate your Microsoft Fabric solution—workspaces, pipelines, notebooks, data agents, models, reports, and more—delivered as a CI/CD‑ready script, ready to deploy.

commit.message:
Fix circuit-sim for Windows: text rawfile + encoding

- parse_rawfile.py: Support both binary and text (Values:) rawfile
  formats. Windows ngspice outputs text format even with -r flag.
- run_sim.py: Add encoding='utf-8' to all NamedTemporaryFile and
  Path.read_text() calls. Fixes UnicodeEncodeError on Windows
  where the default encoding is cp1252.

Tested: all three example netlists (rc_lowpass, pulse_response,
rc_sweep) pass end-to-end on Windows with ngspice 45.2.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

[ci] E: Restrict image sizes to 128 MB

Remove 256, 512, and 1024 MB memory-size variants from the CI
build and release-archive matrices. Only the 128 MB image is now
built and published.

Changes:
- ci-build: replace conditional fromJSON expression with static
  [128] and remove the debug-build exclude entries for 256/512/1024
  that are no longer needed.
- release-archive: narrow memory-size matrix from
  [128, 256, 512, 1024] to [128].

The publish-release action discovers artifacts via glob pattern,
so it adapts automatically with no further changes.

[doc] E: Split troubleshooting guide by platform

Split the monolithic doc/troubleshooting.md into platform-specific
guides:

- doc/troubleshooting-linux.md: Resource leak cleanup, stale build
  cache, CI/CD failures, and TIME_WAIT socket issues.

- doc/troubleshooting-windows.md: WHP enablement, Docker symlink
  errors, stale .venv cleanup, and build artifact cleanup.

The original doc/troubleshooting.md is now a landing page that
directs users to the appropriate platform-specific guide.

[doc] E: Split run guide by platform

Split the monolithic doc/run.md into platform-specific guides:

- doc/run-linux.md: Linux runtime instructions, including interactive
  mode, HTTP mode, container integration, logging, and standalone
  User VM debugging.

- doc/run-windows.md: Windows runtime instructions, including z.ps1
  run, direct nanvixd invocation, logging, and standalone UserVM
  debugging.

The original doc/run.md is now a landing page that directs users
to the appropriate platform-specific guide.

Updated cross-reference in doc/docker-images.md to point to
doc/run-linux.md.

commit.message:
[tests] F: Re-implement C tests in Rust

Port C test suites to idiomatic Rust, advancing issue #1787.

New test crates:
- misc-rust: uid/gid, clock, uname, hostname, nanosleep, times, getenv
- memory-rust: malloc/free, aligned_alloc, realloc, mmap/munmap, heap
- network-rust: INET and Unix domain socket tests
- dlfcn-rust: dlopen/dlclose/dlsym/dladdr (standard and PIE)
- c-bindings-rust: compile-time type size/alignment assertions

Extended test crates:
- file-rust: chmod, datasync, timestamps, link, mkdir, rename, stat,
  symlink (safe side); documented remaining unsafe-side gaps as TODOs
- thread-rust: identity, attributes, mutex dynamic init, mutex trylock,
  mutex timedlock, rwlocks, thread-local storage, cond timedwait;
  documented nowait.c as infeasible with current KernelThread abstraction

Test infrastructure:
- Registered all new suites in test.toml, test-multi_process.toml, and
  test-single_process.toml (both http and terminal executors)
- Added new crates to Cargo.toml workspace members
- Added new binaries to ALL_GUEST_TESTS in Makefile
- Added misc-rust hyperlight feature handling in generic-guest-binaries.mk

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
[docs] docs: add context name guidance to Omni Quick Start (#3350)

* docs: add context name guidance to Omni Quick Start

After `ksail cluster create`, Omni generates its own kubeconfig context
name (e.g., 'devantler-prod') rather than the 'admin@<name>' convention
used by Docker/Hetzner Talos clusters. Guide users to retrieve and set
spec.cluster.connection.context in ksail.yaml to match.
